name: CIB seven - HTTP Connector
description:
Make an HTTP(S) request from a BPMN Service Task using the CIB seven HTTP connector.

HOW TO USE (Camunda 7): apply to a bpmn:ServiceTask. The task's bpmn:extensionElements must contain a <camunda:connector> holding <camunda:connectorId>http-connector</camunda:connectorId> and a <camunda:inputOutput>.

INPUTS (camunda:inputParameter, by name): 'url' = absolute target URL (string or ${expr}), required; 'method' = one of GET, POST, PUT, PATCH, DELETE, HEAD, OPTIONS, TRACE; 'payload' = request body for POST/PUT/PATCH (string or ${expr}); 'headers' = a Map<String,String> of request headers; 'request-config' = an optional Map of HTTP-client options (keys: connection-timeout, response-timeout, redirects-enabled, max-redirects, etc.).

OUTPUTS (camunda:outputParameter; the source expression reads the connector response): name=<your var> source=${statusCode} (Integer), source=${response} (response body String), source=${headers} (response headers Map).

MAPS: this engine uses EL 2.2 which CANNOT evaluate inline map literals - do NOT write ${ {...} }. Provide 'headers' and 'request-config' either as a <camunda:map> with <camunda:entry key="...">value</camunda:entry> children inside the inputParameter, or as ${someVar} referencing a process variable that already holds a java.util.Map.

COMPLETE EXAMPLE (service task body): <camunda:connector><camunda:connectorId>http-connector</camunda:connectorId><camunda:inputOutput><camunda:inputParameter name="method">GET</camunda:inputParameter><camunda:inputParameter name="url">https://httpbin.org/get</camunda:inputParameter><camunda:inputParameter name="headers"><camunda:map><camunda:entry key="Accept">application/json</camunda:entry></camunda:map></camunda:inputParameter><camunda:outputParameter name="statusCode">${statusCode}</camunda:outputParameter><camunda:outputParameter name="response">${response}</camunda:outputParameter></camunda:inputOutput></camunda:connector>

properties.label: URL
properties.description: Absolute target URL, e.g. https://api.example.com/v1/orders. Expressions are allowed, e.g. ${baseUrl}/orders.

properties.label: Headers (map)
properties.description: HTTP request headers as a Map<String,String>. The engine (EL 2.2) does NOT support inline map literals, so do not use ${ {...} }. Supply the map either as ${headersVar} referencing a process variable that holds a Map, or - when emitting BPMN directly - as a <camunda:map> with <camunda:entry key="Content-Type">application/json</camunda:entry> children inside this inputParameter. Leave empty if not needed.

properties.label: Payload (body)
properties.description: Request body for POST/PUT/PATCH. A String or an expression, e.g. ${orderJson}.

properties.label: Request config (map)
properties.description: Optional request-config options as a Map. Inline map literals (${ {...} }) are NOT supported by the engine; supply ${configVar} (a Map process variable) or a <camunda:map> of <camunda:entry> children in the BPMN. Supported keys: authentication-enabled, circular-redirects-allowed, connection-timeout, connection-keep-alive, connection-request-timeout, content-compression-enabled, cookie-spec, expect-continue-enabled, hard-cancellation-enabled, max-redirects, proxy, proxy-preferred-auth-scheme, redirects-enabled, response-timeout, target-preferred-auth-schemes.

properties.label: Status code -> variable
properties.description: Process variable that receives the HTTP status code.

properties.label: Response body -> variable
properties.description: Process variable that receives the response body.

properties.label: Response headers -> variable
properties.description: Process variable that receives the response headers map.
